## CREDENTIAL ARCHITECTURE

### Three-Layer Failover System

```
Layer 1: GSM (Primary)
  ├─ Mechanism: Google Cloud OIDC (Workload Identity Federation)
  ├─ Token TTL: 1 hour (ephemeral)
  ├─ Availability: 99.99% (Google Cloud infrastructure)
  └─ Status: ✅ ACTIVE

Layer 2: Vault (Fallback)
  ├─ Mechanism: AppRole authentication
  ├─ Token TTL: 5-60 minutes (configurable)
  ├─ Availability: Customer-managed
  └─ Status: ✅ READY (manual config of VAULT_ADDR required)

Layer 3: KMS (Emergency)
  ├─ Mechanism: Cloud Key Management Service
  ├─ Token TTL: Manual rotation required
  ├─ Availability: Customer-managed
  └─ Status: ✅ READY (manual config of KMS_KEY_ID required)
```

### Environment Configuration Required

**Step 1: Google Cloud OIDC Setup**
```bash
# Create Workload Identity Pool and Provider
export PROJECT_ID="your-gcp-project"
export LOCATION="global"

gcloud iam workload-identity-pools create "github-pool" \
  --project="$PROJECT_ID" \
  --location="$LOCATION"

gcloud iam workload-identity-pools providers create-oidc "github" \
  --project="$PROJECT_ID" \
  --location="$LOCATION" \
  --attribute-mapping="google.subject=assertion.sub" \
  --issuer-uri="https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
  --pool="github-pool"

# Create Service Account
gcloud iam service-accounts create github-actions \
  --project="$PROJECT_ID" \
  --display-name="GitHub Actions CI/CD"

# Grant permissions (adjust as needed for your environment)
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding "$PROJECT_ID" \
  --member="serviceAccount:github-actions@${PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com" \
  --role="roles/container.serviceAgent"

# Configure workload identity binding
WIP_POOL_ID=$(gcloud iam workload-identity-pools describe github-pool \
  --project="$PROJECT_ID" --location="$LOCATION" --format="value(name)")

gcloud iam service-accounts add-iam-policy-binding \
  github-actions@${PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com \
  --project="$PROJECT_ID" \
  --role="roles/iam.workloadIdentityUser" \
  --member="principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/${WIP_POOL_ID}/attribute.repository/kushin77/self-hosted-runner"
```

**Step 2: Configure GitHub Secrets**
```bash
gh secret set GCP_PROJECT_ID --body "your-gcp-project"
gh secret set GCP_WIF_PROVIDER --body "projects/PROJECT_NUMBER/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/github-pool/providers/github"
gh secret set GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT --body "github-actions@YOUR-PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com"
```

**Step 3: (Optional) Vault Configuration**
```bash
gh secret set VAULT_ADDR --body "https://vault.your-domain.com"
gh secret set VAULT_ROLE_ID --body "<role-id-from-vault>"
gh secret set VAULT_SECRET_ID --body "<secret-id-from-vault>"
```

---

## DEPLOYMENT PROCEDURES

### Staging Deployment

**Command**:
```bash
gh workflow run .github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml \
  --ref production-handoff-manifest
```

**Expected Duration**: 30-45 minutes

**Validation Steps**:
1. Monitor workflow: `gh run list --workflow deploy-staging.yml`
2. Verify services health: `kubectl get pods -n staging`
3. Run smoke tests: `./scripts/verify-production-ready.sh`
4. Check metrics: CloudAudit logs in GCP Console

**Success Criteria**:
- ✅ All pods running (READY 1/1)
- ✅ gitleaks scan green
- ✅ Container security scan clean
- ✅ Integration tests passing
- ✅ Metrics ingesting to Datadog
- ✅ Disaster recovery drills successful

### Production Deployment

**Command** (after staging validation):
```bash
gh workflow run .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml \
  --ref main
```

**Expected Duration**: 45-60 minutes

**Pre-Deployment Checklist**:
- [ ] Staging deployment successful
- [ ] On-call engineer acknowledged
- [ ] Maintenance window scheduled (if required)
- [ ] Rollback plan reviewed
- [ ] All documentation current

**During Deployment**:
- Monitor: `gh run list --workflow deploy-production.yml`
- Watch: CloudAudit logs for any errors
- Alert: PagerDuty escalation if issues detected
- Validate: Health checks via Sentinel dashboards

**Post-Deployment Validation**:
- ✅ All replicas deployed and healthy
- ✅ Traffic flowing through all services
- ✅ Metrics stable and normal
- ✅ Error rates < baseline threshold
- ✅ Incident response teams standing by

---

## ROLLBACK PROCEDURE

### Quick Rollback (< 5 minutes)
```bash
# Identify last working commit
git log --oneline | head -5

# Trigger rollback workflow to specific commit
gh workflow run .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml \
  --ref <previous-commit-sha>
```

### Full Rollback to Previous Release
```bash
# List releases
gh release list

# Rollback to previous release
gh workflow run .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml \
  --ref v.2026-03-07-final  # Previous working version
```

### Emergency Stop
```bash
# Immediately scale down all deployments
kubectl scale deployment --all --replicas=0 -n production

# Verify scaled down
kubectl get deployments -n production

# Manual intervention required - page on-call engineer
```

---

## MONITORING & ALERTING

### Key Metrics to Monitor
| Metric | Threshold | Alert Level |
|--------|-----------|------------|
| Pod Health | < 90% ready | 🔴 CRITICAL |
| Error Rate | > 1% | 🟡 WARNING |
| Latency P95 | > 2s | 🟡 WARNING |
| CPU Usage | > 80% | 🟡 WARNING |
| Memory Usage | > 85% | 🔴 CRITICAL |
| Secret Rotation Failures | > 0 | 🔴 CRITICAL |

### Viewing Dashboards
```bash
# CloudAudit logs
gcloud logging read "resource.type=k8s_cluster" --limit 50

# Datadog dashboard
# https://app.datadoghq.com/dash/integration/kubernetes

# Prometheus metrics
kubectl port-forward -n prometheus svc/prometheus 9090:9090
# Then access: http://localhost:9090
```

---

## INCIDENT RESPONSE

### On-Call Escalation Path
1. **Tier 1**: Automated alerts (PagerDuty)
2. **Tier 2**: On-call engineer (page if not acknowledged in 5 min)
3. **Tier 3**: Engineering manager (page if not resolved in 30 min)
4. **Tier 4**: Platform team lead (page if not resolved in 60 min)

### Contact Information
- **On-Call Schedule**: PagerDuty (setup required)
- **Incident Channel**: #production-incidents (Slack)
- **War Room**: Jitsi (auto-created in incident ticket)

### Common Issues & Fixes

**Issue**: Pod CrashLoopBackOff
```bash
# Get logs
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n production

# Get pod events
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n production

# Scale down and debug
kubectl scale deployment <deployment> --replicas=1
```

**Issue**: Secret Rotation Failed
```bash
# Check Vault connectivity
./scripts/fetch-credentials.sh validate

# Force manual rotation
./scripts/fetch-credentials.sh rotate_credentials

# Check GSM fallback
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=<secret-name>
```

**Issue**: High Error Rate
```bash
# Check logs
kubectl logs --tail=100 -l app=your-app -n production

# Check metrics
# Access Datadog dashboard for detailed analysis

# Scale up if resource-constrained
kubectl scale deployment <deployment> --replicas=<n>
```
